melfs-minute-meteors.canonicalEvidence: When you cast the spell - and as a bonus action on each of your turns thereafter - you can expend one or two of the meteors.
melfs-minute-meteors.structuredState: No initial spend plus later bonus action spend fields.
melfs-minute-meteors.issue: Melfs Minute Meteors has an initial spend and repeat bonus-action spend loop that is not represented.
melfs-minute-meteors.recommendedTemplateChange: Add initialAndSustainAction fields for expendable spell instances.
melfs-minute-meteors.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime initialSpend plus bonus_action sustain spend 1-2 meteors.

melfs-minute-meteors.canonicalEvidence: Once a meteor reaches its destination or impacts against a solid surface, the meteor explodes.
melfs-minute-meteors.structuredState: No solid surface impact trigger field.
melfs-minute-meteors.issue: Meteors explode on destination arrival or solid-surface impact, but that trigger is not represented.
melfs-minute-meteors.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime projectileTrigger for destination reached or solid surface impact.

melfs-minute-meteors.canonicalEvidence: You create six tiny meteors in your space. They float in the air and orbit you for the spell duration.
melfs-minute-meteors.jsonState: Runtime JSON has no orbiting meteor objects or charges.
melfs-minute-meteors.issue: The six created orbiting meteors are not represented as expendable created instances.
melfs-minute-meteors.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime createdInstances for six orbiting meteors in caster space.

melfs-minute-meteors.canonicalEvidence: They float in the air and orbit you for the spell duration.
melfs-minute-meteors.jsonState: Runtime JSON has no persistent orbiting state.
melfs-minute-meteors.issue: The orbiting meteors are a persistent created-object state, tracked in the created-object row rather than as a separate environmental mechanic.
melfs-minute-meteors.recommendedTemplateChange: No environmental field required for this row beyond created-instance support.
melfs-minute-meteors.recommendedJsonChange: No separate environmental runtime change required.

motivational-speech.canonicalEvidence: Choose up to five creatures within range that can hear you.
motivational-speech.issue: The up-to-five target count is represented; the hearing requirement is tracked separately.
motivational-speech.recommendedTemplateChange: No target-count change required for this row.
motivational-speech.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by correcting Targeting Max and runtime maxTargets from 6 to 5.

motivational-speech.canonicalEvidence: Each affected creature gains 5 temporary hit points and has advantage on Wisdom saving throws. If an affected creature is hit by an attack, it has advantage on the next attack roll it makes.
motivational-speech.structuredState: Effect Type: BUFF; no save advantage or next attack advantage fields.
motivational-speech.jsonState: Runtime prose was corrected, but no typed save or attack-roll modifier exists.
motivational-speech.issue: Motivational Speech grants Wisdom save advantage and a conditional next attack-roll advantage after being hit, but both are prose-only.
motivational-speech.recommendedTemplateChange: Add save advantage and hit-triggered next attack advantage fields.
motivational-speech.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime saveModifier Wisdom advantage and hit-triggered nextAttackAdvantage.

motivational-speech.canonicalEvidence: Choose up to five creatures within range that can hear you.
motivational-speech.jsonState: Runtime targeting does not require hearing.
motivational-speech.issue: The spell targets creatures that can hear the caster, which lineOfSight cannot represent.

motivational-speech.canonicalEvidence: You address allies, staff, or innocent bystanders to exhort and inspire them to greatness.
motivational-speech.structuredState: No social payload field beyond BUFF.
motivational-speech.jsonState: Runtime JSON keeps this as prose only.
motivational-speech.issue: The exhortation presentation is mostly descriptive; the mechanical hearing requirement and buffs are tracked in other rows.
motivational-speech.recommendedTemplateChange: No separate social mechanic field required for this row.
motivational-speech.recommendedJsonChange: No separate social runtime change required.

motivational-speech.canonicalEvidence: Each affected creature gains 5 temporary hit points. The temporary hit points increase by 5 for each slot level above 3.
motivational-speech.structuredState: Effect Type: BUFF; no Temporary Hit Points amount or scaling fields.
motivational-speech.jsonState: Runtime prose mentions Temporary Hit Points, but no typed temporary HP effect exists.
motivational-speech.issue: Motivational Speech grants and scales temporary hit points, but runtime has no typed temporary HP effect.
motivational-speech.recommendedTemplateChange: Add temporary hit point amount and scaling fields.
motivational-speech.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime TEMPORARY_HIT_POINTS effect value 5 with +5 per slot above 3.

motivational-speech.canonicalEvidence: Once an affected creature loses the temporary hit points granted by this spell, the spell ends for that creature.
motivational-speech.structuredState: No per-target ending tied to granted temp HP loss.
motivational-speech.jsonState: Runtime JSON has no conditional ending data.
motivational-speech.issue: The spell ends per target when its granted temporary HP are lost, but that ending rule is not represented.
motivational-speech.recommendedTemplateChange: Add per-target ending fields tied to granted temporary HP depletion.
motivational-speech.closedReason: Reused the existing temporary_hit_points_depleted conditional-ending trigger, recorded it in the structured spell fields, and mapped it to the runtime utility effect with effect scope for each affected creature.
